person.name: Margaret de Orrell

burialPlace.address: Market Harborough, Bowden Magna, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Margaret de Langton (born Orrell) was born in 1302, in unknown birth place , to William III Orrell and Sibil Orrell (born de Pierrepont) .
William was born in 1275, in Newton-le-Willows, Makerfield, Lancashire, England.
Sibil was born in 1292, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England.
Margaret married Robert de Langton .
Robert was born in 1298, in West Langton, Leicestershire, England.
They had 2 children: John de Langton and one other child .
Margaret passed away in 1340, at age 38 in unknown death place .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Orrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from either of two minor places in Lancashire called Orell. The placename derives from Old English ōra ‘bank, slope, hill, edge’ or ōra ‘ore’ + hyll ‘hill’.

variants.name: Orrill
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire and Leicestershire): variant of   Orrell  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Orrell was born in 1302, in Northamptonshire, England as the daughter of William de Orrell. She had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Robert de Langston. She died in 1340, in Leicestershire, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in Market Harborough, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Orrellは1302年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアでWilliam de Orrellの娘として生まれた。彼女はRobert de Langstonとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1340年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアで38歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ハーボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Orrell(은)는 1302년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 William de Orrell의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert de Langston(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1340년 영국 레이스스터실르에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 마크트 하르버루그h에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Orrell родилась в 1302 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия. Её отца звали William de Orrell. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Robert de Langston. Она умерла в 1340 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности маркет харбороую, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
